sentences.text: You've noticed it on the bakery site: you tell Claude your brand colors in one chat, then a week later, in a fresh session, you have to say them again.
Claude reads the whole conversation each time, but it doesn't carry your preferences between separate projects unless you write them down somewhere it looks.
The fix is a small, plain-language file that lives in your project — a place Claude reads automatically so your rules are always in the room.
